headline: LMT Q1 Deep Dive: Backlog Growth and Production Challenges Shape Outlook
summary: Security and Aerospace company Lockheed Martin (NYSE:LMT) missed Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales flat year on year at $18.02 billion. On the other hand, the company’s outlook for the full year was close to analysts’ estimates with revenue guided to $78.75 billion at the midpoint. Its GAAP profit of $6.44 per share was 3.8% below analysts’ consensus estimates.

raw.headline: LMT Q1 Deep Dive: Backlog Growth and Production Challenges Shape Outlook
raw.summary: Security and Aerospace company Lockheed Martin (NYSE:LMT) missed Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales flat year on year at $18.02 billion. On the other hand, the company’s outlook for the full year was close to analysts’ estimates with revenue guided to $78.75 billion at the midpoint. Its GAAP profit of $6.44 per share was 3.8% below analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Defense Stocks Selloff Deepens. The Sector Can Survive a ‘Blue Wave.’
summary: The selloff in defense stocks has gotten out of hand, according to Citi analyst John Godyn.  Coming into Friday trading, shares of  Lockheed Martin Northrop Grumman General Dynamics RTX  and  L3Harris Technologies  were down 14% since the Iran war started and 8% since Monday.  “In just a few weeks, the top-down narrative on defense stocks has completely flip-flopped,” wrote Godyn on Thursday.

headline: Leveraged ETFs Explained: 3 Picks for Active Traders Who Want Speed
summary: Leveraged exchange-traded funds deliver a daily multiple of an underlying index’s return, using swaps and futures to reset exposure every session. The daily reset means multi-day performance depends on the path the index travels between the start and end points. Traders use these instruments to express short-horizon directional views over brief holding periods. Context shapes ... Leveraged ETFs Explained: 3 Picks for Active Traders Who Want Speed

headline: Lockheed Martin Peru F-16 Deal And GPS III Launch Reshape Growth Story
summary: Lockheed Martin (NYSE:LMT) secured a direct commercial sale of F-16 Block 70 fighter jets to the Peruvian Air Force, marking the first such F-16 commercial deal in decades. The agreement expands Lockheed Martin's fighter aircraft presence in South America and deepens U.S.–Peru defense ties. Separately, the company supported the launch of the GPS III SV10 satellite, a step in its ongoing work on GPS III and future GPS IIIF systems. For investors watching NYSE:LMT, these moves add fresh...
